channel.title: Ryan E. Wong
channel.description: Recent content on Ryan E. Wong

item.title: d-gen
item.description: Updated on December 10, 2020 d-gen is a project that I developed as part of the 2020 CDAC Summer Lab under the mentorship of Professor Raul Castro-Ferenandez. The purpose of d-gen is to synthetically generate relational databases that adhere to a user-defined schema for the purposes of query testing and optimization. Relational databases are databases which contain tables filled with columns and rows of information, which may or may not hold some sort of “relation” to each other.

item.title: Xtract Container Service (XCS)
item.description: Updated on December 11, 2020 The Xtract Container Service (XCS) is a service I developed as a research assistant at Globus Labs under the mentorship of Tyler Skluzacek. XCS is a service for building and storing containers (the computing kind, not the storage kind), and is part of the greater XtractHub project. Xtract’s main feature is its ability to process heterogeneous data in a scalable manner. To do so, it uses FuncX, which utilizes containers, allowing for the large-scale execution of various functions on the same computing device.

item.title: Xtract Classifier
item.description: Updated on December 11, 2020 The Xtract Classifier is a machine learning tool that I developed as a research at Globus Labs under the mentorship of Tyler Skluzacek. The Xtract Classifier is an experimental tool designed to categorize files, and is part of the greater XtractHub project. One of Xtract’s main features is its ability to process heterogeneous data. It does so by using “extractors”, which are programs hosted inside containers, which can be used to extract metadata from specific types of files.

item.title: XtractHub
item.description: Updated on December 16, 2020 XtractHub is a project that I contributed to as part of the 2019 CDAC Summer Lab under the mentorship of Tyler Skluzacek. Xtract is a service that extracts metadata from heterogeneous scientific file formats, and is a project from Globus Labs. As advancements in scientific computing have been made, the volume and variety of data produced by experiments have increased significantly. Most of this scientific data is stored in public/shared repositories such as Petrel.

item.title: About Me
item.description: I’m currently a high scool senior at Whitney Young HS in Chicago. I’ve been a member of my school’s cross country and track team since 8th grade as well as a member of my school’s orchestra. Outside of school, I’ve been a member of the UChicago STEM Initiative (as part of UChicago’s Office of Special Programs) since 2018. I’ve also held multiple research assistant positions at UChicago’s Center for Data and Computing and Globus Labs since 2019.
